betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Right at the start of HBM’s Nuclear Tech Mod, things kick off here.§a Getting ready means §bgathering lots of iron, coal, aluminium, copper - redstone too.§f Skip this step only if running low doesn’t scare you. (lmao)

Not every tool comes straight from a factory. §6Some high-end gear §fshows up when you§6 explore old structures found almost anywhere on the map - Centrifuges, Blast furnaces, and many more.

§6Machine Template Folder§f is a utility item used to create §btemplates§f, §bstamps§f, and §bfluid identifiers§f for various machines.

§bTemplates§f define §6recipes§f for §bcrucibles§f and are also used by §bassembly machines§f and §bchemical plants§f. Without the correct template, these machines §cwill not operate§f.

§bStamps§f are consumable tools used in §bpresses§f. They have §elimited durability§f, which depends on the material used. Higher-tier materials last longer, up to §6desh stamps§f, which are §aunbreakable§f.

§bFluid Identifiers§f act as §6filters§f for §bducts§f, §bbarrels§f, and multiple machines. Later progression allows replacing standard identifiers with a §6multi-fluid identifier§f to handle more complex setups.

§eThe folder includes a §9search bar§e used to select what you want to craft.§f If the required materials are present, the item is §ainserted directly into your inventory§f.

§eCrafting rules:
§b• Templates§f require §6paper§f and §bany dye§f. They can be §arecycled back into paper§f.
§b• Fluid Identifiers§f require an §6iron plate§f and §bany dye§f.
§b• Stamps§f are crafted using their §6corresponding blank stamp§f.

§6Anvils§f §c(not the vanilla kind)§f are §6tiered crafting stations§f used to create §bmachines§f, §bstructures§f, and select §bitems§f. Each higher tier unlocks §emore recipes§f, though some tiers currently act only as §eprogression steps§f without added functionality.

§eAnvil tiers roughly follow the mod’s §6material progression§f, making them a §auseful indicator§f of what you should work toward next. They are §ccompletely separate§f from vanilla anvils and §cshare none of their mechanics§f.

§6Crafting:§:

§e§bIron§e and §bLead Anvils§e can be crafted directly from their §6blocks§f and §6ingots§f. Higher-tier anvils require §610 ingots§f of the new material combined with an existing §bIron or Lead Anvil§f inside another anvil’s §9GUI§f.

§eEach upgrade replaces the previous anvil§f, gradually advancing your crafting capabilities as new materials become available.

§eOne press at NTM runs on §belectricity§e, another uses §bhydraulic force§e, while the third relies on §bpneumatic energy§e — each built for the same job.§f Machines differ only by §6their power source.

§eBullets can be stamped from presses§f, just like §bthin metal sheets§f or §bwires. §eShaping unsmeltable powder is another job they handle — no melting needed.§f

§6Burner Press§f

§eAfter fuel enters the press, it gets used up along with §6saved actions§f. §eWaiting quietly, the machine stays idle without the right §bstamp§e and §bmaterial§e together.§f Only then does anything happen next.

§eOnce started, the machine keeps going until all material is pressed§f, gradually §amoving faster§f. §eBecause of this pattern, handling §6several pieces together§e works better.§f If only §cone item§f goes through at a time, §cmomentum drops sharply§f§c, and you waste fuel.§

§cBesides being §ctough,§f§c these bugs come in several forms. Hives§f§c pop up on their own across the terrain,§f bringing them into play. §cTheir shells reduce attack damage§f compared to regular creatures. §cThey are attracted to pollution.§f Dealing with swarms of these creatures tends to fall on whoever caused the disturbance.

§6Hives:
§f§cA cluster might pop up by chance anywhere §aexcept ocean zones,§f though§c scout creatures sometimes build them too.§f Inside, you will find a§c few egg clusters§f along with §cbasic items§f just sitting there among the pods.§c When those egg blocks break apart, they release bugs plus leave behind loose eggs.§f§a Most other parts of the nest vanish without trace if destroyed.§f There are two types:

§6Normal Hives:
§aHive blocks with §bred marks§a hold regular hives. Those usual nests produce§c standard glyphids.§f From them, typical creatures emerge without exception.

§6Parasitic Hives:§a
A strange type of hive pops up now and then -§b covered in pale blotches that might be parasites clinging under thick shells.§a Strike one, and it fights back with §cpoision and nauseatic effects.§f Once broken open, blood sprays out while §ctwo to four worm-like young crawl free.§f §aThe structure seems washed out, duller than normal, with patches of muddy brown scattered across its surface.

§6Nuclear Integrated Tech Research : Overhauled§f is a §bHBM-NTMCE-focused§f modpack that is in a §eheavy reworking stage§f, aiming to §aintroduce extensive microcrafting§f to help players §6advance through automated crafting chains§f built using §bvarious machines§f from different mods.

§fThis pack was created with the goal of introducing §erealism§f in the §6nuclear-focused§f progression, while §acarefully integrating§f multiple other mods into a cohesive experience.

§eIn the future, I plan to add §6hypothetical machinery§f that exists mostly in theory, such as §bDyson Spheres§f and possibly the §bPenrose Process§f for §6Black Hole energy extraction§f. §aSuggestions are welcome§f on my Discord server.

§fAll said, I wish you §2all the best§f as you begin your journey in this world!

§c(NOTE: Many quests are not yet implemented due to time constraints. You are encouraged to freely explore and test all aspects of the modpack, including exploration, technology, and crafting.)

§6Essential Tools§f play a major role in progressing through the mod. While many exist, a few are §6critical to survival and advancement§f.

§bTungsten Reachers§f reduce §cradiation exposure§f from items carried in your inventory and allow you to §ahandle extremely hot materials§f safely.

§bCrowbar§f is used to §6open sealed crates§f found in structures, yielding loot such as §bfood§f and §bammunition§f.

§bScrewdriver§f serves multiple purposes. It can §6rotate conveyor blocks§f, is required to §6construct a Chicago Pile§f, and—when combined with §bBobby Pins§f—can be used to §aunlock safes§f hidden throughout the world.

§bArmor Modification Table§f allows you to §6install upgrades§f into armor. The most important modification is §6Cladding§f, which §areduces radiation intake§f. Most armor modifications are §efound in chests§f or can be §ecrafted§f.

§6Blast Furnace§f is a §6fuel-powered alloy furnace§f used to combine §btwo materials§f into advanced metals. It runs on §blava§f or specific §bsolid fuels§f.

§eThe furnace accepts §btwo compatible inputs§f placed in the §etop and bottom slots§f — §aorder does not matter§f. Fuel is inserted into the §eright-side slot§f. Without valid fuel and inputs, the machine §cwill not run§f.

§eInputs may be provided as §bingots§f, §bsolid items§f, or §bpowders§f. However, §acombining powders in a crafting grid first§f is §6more fuel-efficient§f, allowing the alloyed powder to be smelted using other methods.

§eWhile the Blast Furnace has §erelatively few recipes§f, most are §6critical for progression§f:

§b• Steel§f = §6Iron Ore§f + §6Coal§f  
§b• Steel§f = §6Iron§f + §6Coal§f (§eproduces 1 ingot§f)  
§b• Red Copper§f = §6Copper§f + §6Redstone§f  
§b• Advanced Alloy§f = §6Steel§f + §6Red Copper§f

§cBlack Lung, or silicosis, is formed upon mining a coal ore.§f When this happens, §cCoal Dust is spawned§f, which can wreak havoc on your lungs, causing you to suffer §cweakness§f and begin §ccoughing blood and soot§f.

§aProtecting yourself from these deadly dust particles is quite simple however.§f All you need is a §6simple-to-craft Trench Mask§f.

§eCloth can be created from §bwool§e and §bstring§e in the crafting table, and then §9held and right-clicked§e to §curinate on them§f, resulting in §burinated cloth§f. Using this, you can then §6create a Trench Mask§f.

§eGas masks having an §6Airborne Particles§e trait (also including the §bTrench Mask§e) will §aprotect you from coal dust§f.  
§9(Use the Armor Modification Table to equip a filter to the gas mask once crafted)§f

§aUsing §bSiOX Cancer Medication§a will reset the disease to §e25%§a, from which it will heal in around §e10 minutes§f.

§6Hazmat Suits§f are protective armor sets designed to §6shield the player from hazardous environments§f, most notably §cradiation§f. Higher protection tiers can be achieved by reinforcing §bHazmat Cloth§f, allowing the creation of §6more resistant suits§f and eventually §bLiquidator Suits§f.

§eFor protection against airborne dangers§f, a §bGas Mask Filter§f is required. §9Left-click§f with the filter to equip it. Without a filter installed, §cgas-based hazards will still affect you§f even while wearing protective gear.

§6Blast Furnace Extension§f is an upgrade block placed §eon top of the Blast Furnace§f to §6increase processing speed§f.

§eWithout the extension§f, the Blast Furnace runs at about §chalf the speed§f§c of a normal furnace. §eWith the extension installed§f, processing speed is §6tripled§f.

§aFuel usage does not increase§f, meaning the extension also §6triples fuel efficiency§f, making it a §abest-in-slot upgrade§f for alloy production.

§6Power Transmission§f starts with §bRed Copper Cables§f, the most basic way to move electricity. They come in §6two forms§f: a §bfull block§f and a §bthin cable§f. The block version also has a §apaintable variant§f, allowing it to §eblend into builds§f more easily.

§eFor cleaner layouts and §6long-distance power transfer§f, §bCable Drums§f paired with §bElectricity Connectors§f can be used. These powerlines are §anon-solid§f and can §apass through floors and walls§f, making them ideal for compact or vertical builds.

§bCapacitors§f function as §6directional power buffers§f. They §astore energy§f like standard storage blocks but also act as §6diodes§f, allowing power to flow in §eonly one direction§f. Any excess power that isn’t consumed by machines or storage is §aretained§f instead of being wasted.

The Pollution Detector will monitor and show the levels of pollution present in the environment. High levels of pollution will decrease the visibility, give debuffs to the player and buff mobs, including skeletons spawning with firearms.

§6Wood-Burning Generator§f is the §6simplest power source§f available and requires §ano extra components§f or system knowledge to operate. It is intended as an §eentry-level generator§f for early progression.

§eThe generator accepts §bany burnable item§f, functioning similarly to a furnace. §aLogs and wooden blocks§f receive §6burn-time bonuses§f, making them the most efficient solid fuels for this machine.

§eIt can also burn §bflammable fluids§f by clicking the §9arrow below the on/off switch§f. §cThis mode is highly inefficient§f compared to dedicated fluid-based generators and is best used §eonly in emergencies§f.

§6Analog Circuit Board§f is the §6first true circuit tier§f used in NTM. It establishes the §egeneral structure§f of all later circuits, combining §blead wiring§f with §bcommon electronic components§f such as §bcapacitors§f and §bPCBs§f.

§eUnlike later circuits§f, the Analog Circuit §cdoes not require a lower-tier circuit§f to craft. This makes it the §astarting point§f for all electronic progression.

§eAnalog Circuits are required for §6essential machines and tools§f, including the §bOil Deposit Detector§f, §bChemical Plant§f, and §bElectric Arc Furnace§f. These machines unlock components that are §ccritical for higher-tier circuits§f.

§aIf a §bShredder§f is owned§f, a §6cheaper recipe§f for §bCapacitors§f becomes available, reducing the cost of early circuit production.

This iteration of the Questbook was made by §6copernicium282§f (§bir192m2§f on Discord).

§eNotes:§f

§e1) §cThis questbook holds little on §bNTM-CE§c and other mods§f — §echeck JEI§f for details about §bweapons§f, §barmors§f, and similar additions. §eExploration there reveals what's missing here.§f

§e2) §cUpdating the modpack?§f §cIf new versions changed the questbook§f, remove the §6betterquesting§f folder from the §eworld file§f. §cQuest changes can cause old data to clash§f, so wiping it prevents issues.
§eOnce removed§f, §aenable cheats§f and type §9"/bq_admin edit true"§f. Go to any chapter you prefer, find the §edesigner icon§f (a grid of small boxes), select the quest, and §aconfirm it§f to mark completed quests again.§f

§a§bBig shout-out to the NTM Wiki team§f — details pulled from their pages helped shape the quest content.

§6Assembly Machine§f (Assembler) is a §6core fabrication machine§f used to manufacture §bnearly all complex machines§f throughout every stage of the game. It functions as a §63×3 pseudo-multiblock§f and is §cimpossible to skip§f in progression.

§eThe Assembly Machine operates using an §6Assembly Template§f, which defines the recipe and §eassembly time§f. Once a template is selected, the machine will assemble the product over the §especified duration§f shown in the recipe.

§6Recipe Selection:§f

§eRecipes are chosen using the §bTemplate Folder button§f in the §9GUI§f. Selecting a recipe displays a §epreview of required inputs§f before assembly begins.

§eSome advanced recipes additionally require §6Blueprints§f to be inserted into the blueprint slot. Notable blueprint-locked recipes include:
§b• Gerald the Construction Android§f  
§b• Radiation-Powered Engine§f  
§b• All Plates§f  
§b• Soyuz-FG§f  
§b• Orbital Module§f  
§b• .50 BMG Round (Starmetal)§f  

§eBlueprints are created using §bBlueprint Booklets§f found in world structures. Using a booklet §arandomly generates§f a blueprint.

§6Efficiency Notes§f

§cAlthough the Assembly Machine can produce wires and plates§f, it is §cnot recommended§f for this purpose. §ePresses§f yield §ahigher output§f and better efficiency.

§eAdditionally§f, some Assembly Machine recipes have §eexact equivalents§f on §bAnvils§f, including certain §bupgrades§f and §6improved wire or plate recipes§f. Choosing the correct machine §aoptimizes material usage§f.

§6Shredder§f is a processing machine used to §6pulverize resources§f into §bpowders§f or, in many cases, their §bbase crafting materials§f. It also serves as an §eearly-game ore doubling§f method.

§eThe Shredder operates with §62 Shredder Blades§f installed and requires §belectric power§f to function. Each operation consumes approximately §6300 HE§f.

§eThe machine features §69 input slots§f and processes §aall inserted items simultaneously§f. Different items can be shredded at the same time, and doing so is §arecommended§f as it §6reduces power usage§f, §6processing time§f, and §6blade wear§f compared to single-item runs.

§eWhen §bore§f is shredded§f, it produces §62× dust§f of that material, which can be smelted into §62 ingots§f. While this method is §einferior to the §bCentrifuge§f§f for late-game processing, it is §aexcellent for early progression§f and for handling §esignificant volumes§f of one or more ores efficiently.

§6Soldering Station§f is a §6key multiblock machine§f used to §6assemble circuits§f and §bmanufacture machine upgrades§f. It serves as the §ecentral workspace§f for all circuit-related progression.

§eIts first major use§f is crafting §6Analog Circuit Boards§f, which are required by §bearly-game machines§f and systems. As progression continues, the Soldering Station becomes §aessential§f for producing §6advanced circuits§f and §bupgrade components§f that improve machine performance.

§6Crucible§f is the §6core machine of metallurgy§f in NTM. It handles §6ore processing§f, §balloying§f, and §bmetal casting§f. Some alloys are §amore efficient§f or §cexclusive§f to this machine, though a few can be produced §eslower§f using a §bBlast Furnace§f.

§eThe Crucible requires an §6external heat source§f to function. It must be placed §eon top of a heat-producing machine§f and will operate as long as it receives at least §650,000 TU§f.

§cWhile operating§f, the Crucible emits a §cconstant amount of soot§f, creating §6unavoidable pollution§f that §ccannot be mitigated§f. Running it without proper exhaust planning §cwill contaminate the area§f.

§eThe machine has §6two output sides§f:
§a• Green side§f – §eAccepts metals valid for the selected recipe§f and §amixes them correctly§f.  
§c• Red side§f – §eCollects all other materials as §cwaste§f.

§cIf no recipe template is installed§f, §call materials flow into the waste side§f.

§6Arc Welder§f is a §6core fabrication machine§f used to produce §bstructural components§f such as §bcast plates§f, §bmissile parts§f, and other advanced materials. Some recipes additionally require a §bfluid input§f to function.

§6Usage & Operation:§f

§eThe Arc Welder features §63 item input slots§f and §61 fluid slot§f. Depending on the recipe, fluids may be §eoptional§f or §cmandatory§f. Each recipe also defines its own §6power consumption§f (measured in §bHE/t§f) and §6processing time§f (measured in §bticks§f).

§6Input / Output Configuration:§f

§eItem slots are §6color-coded:§f
§c• Red slot§f  
§e• Yellow slot§f  
§a• Green slot§f  

§eThese colors correspond directly to the welder’s external ports:§f
§c• 4 Red ports§f  
§a• 4 Green ports§f  
§e• 2 Yellow ports§f  

§eItems must enter through the matching port color§f. §bFluids§f may be inserted through §aany port§f.

§6Primary Uses:§f

§bWelded Plates§f are one of the most important outputs. Each welded plate is made from §62 cast plates§f (each worth §63 ingots§f), totaling §66 ingots per plate§f. These plates are §6mandatory§f for machines such as:
§b• Oil Refinery§f  
§b• Watz Power Plant§f  
§b• Fusion Reactor§f  

§eThe Arc Welder also provides §asimplified recipes§f for frequently used components like §bcircuits§f and §bmotor assemblies§f.

§eAdditionally§f, it is the §6primary machine§f used for assembling §bgeneric missiles§f, making it essential for both §6industrial§f and §cweapon§f progression.

§6Combination Oven§f is used to §6burn and coke materials§f, converting solid inputs into §busable fuel products§f. For example, inserting §bsawdust briquettes§f will produce §6Charcoal§f while generating §bWood Oil§f as a fluid byproduct.

§eSeveral fluid byproducts can be reused§f to create §aself-sustaining setups§f. By piping outputs into a §bFluid Burner§f connected back to the oven, fuel loops can be established. Common outputs include §6Wood Oil§f and §6Coal Tar Creosote§f.

§eLike the §bCrucible§f and §bStirling Engine§f, the Combination Oven requires a §6heat source below§f to operate. Valid heat providers include:
§b• Firebox / Heating Oven§f  
§b• Electric Heater§f  
§b• Fluid Burner§f

§aUsing a Heating Oven is recommended§f over a Firebox due to its §6faster heat generation§f and §6larger heat buffer§f, allowing the Combination Oven to run §asmoother and without interruptions§f.

§cWhile active§f, the Combination Oven produces a §ccolumn of fire§f above itself, igniting blocks and entities within a §62×3×3 area§f. §eEmbers emitted during operation§f §ado not ignite flammable blocks§f.

§6Chemical Plant§f is a §6core processing machine§f used for §6fluid conversion§f and §bchemical synthesis§f. It is required for producing many §badvanced compounds§f throughout progression.

§eThe machine features §63 fluid input tanks§f (each holding §624,000 mB§f) and §63 item input slots§f. To function, it must be §apowered with electricity§f and configured with a §6Chemistry Template§f.

§eOperation is straightforward§f: select the appropriate template from the §9GUI§f, then supply the required §bfluids§f and §bitems§f either manually or via §bfluid ducts§f and §bconveyors§f. Without a valid template, the machine §cwill not run§f.

§eAs with the §bAssembly Machine§f, some recipes additionally require §6Blueprints§f to unlock, making the Chemical Plant a §6key checkpoint§f in mid-game automation.

§aHere: enjoy the old chem plant as a reward...

§6Strand Caster§f is a §6large-scale casting basin§f designed for §abatch production§f. Unlike standard foundry basins, it casts §69 items at once§f, making it §asignificantly faster§f for high-throughput metal processing.

§eThe Strand Caster only activates once fully loaded§f. When all §69 slots§f are filled, it §6casts the entire batch simultaneously§f, greatly reducing downtime compared to smaller casting setups.

§eCasting requires §bwater§f, which is consumed to produce §bLow-Pressure Steam§f during operation. This steam can be §arecycled back into water§f using condensers, allowing efficient §aclosed-loop casting systems§f.

§6High-Speed Steel§f is an §6advanced steel alloy§f used primarily in §bspecialized machines§f and §bhigh-end components§f. It appears in key recipes such as the §bOil Derrick§f and §bIndustrial Mixer§f, marking it as a §6mid-to-late progression material§f.

§cHigh-Speed Steel cannot be used§f to craft §enormal tools or armor§f. Its applications are §especific but important§f, and production is §aworth the effort§f due to what it unlocks.

§eThis material enables the §6battery-powered electric tool family§f:
§b• Stunstick§f (§eelectric sword§f)  
§b• Electric Chainsaw§f (§eelectric axe§f)  
§b• Impact Drill§f (§eelectric pickaxe§f)  
§b• Spiral Drill§f (§eelectric shovel§f)

§eIn processing chains§f, §bHigh-Speed Steel Powder§f is used to create §6Saturnite§f, while the §bIngot form§f can be combined with §6Desh§f and §bShredded Polymer§f to produce §6Desh Compound Plates§f.

§bHigh-Speed Steel Bolts§f are required alongside a §bPneumatic Rivet Gun§f to construct §6major power infrastructure§f, including the §bWatz Power Plant§f and the §bInertial Confinement Fusion Reactor (END GAME)§f.

§6Oil Reservoir Detector§f is the §6primary early-game tool§f used to locate §boil deposits§f. When used, it reports results §edirectly in chat§f, indicating whether oil is nearby or directly below you.

§eTo extract oil§f, you must first locate a valid §boil reservoir§f. In early progression, §cthe Oil Reservoir Detector is the only device capable of doing this§f.

§eUsing the detector§f:
§9Right-click§f anywhere to scan a §6thin vertical column§f beneath your position. The scan range is §cvery small§f (about §68 blocks§f), so effective searching requires §emoving chunk by chunk§f while scanning repeatedly.

§ePossible results include:
§b• “Oil detected nearby”§f – §eOil is close; continue scanning the surrounding area,§f  
§b• “Oil deposit directly below!”§f – §aYou are positioned correctly to extract.§f

§eIf a deposit is found far from your base§f, it can be §amined and relocated§f to a §bstationary Oil Refinery§f for easier processing.

§cNote§f: §eOily dirt or sand patches§f found on the surface §cdo not indicate extractable oil§f. These mark §ebedrock deposits§f, which require §cdifferent extraction methods§f. §eRemember their locations§f for later progression.

§eItems travel easily using §bconveyors§e. These belts §ado not cost much§f yet §eneed careful arrangement§f, thus makeing them §6ideal for automation.

§6Conveyor Belts§f:

§eA §bsingle path§e, §btwo paths§e, or §bthree paths§e of moving belts can be made.§f Each one lines up with §bpushers§f aimed exactly where things feed in.

§eThings move on conveyor belts.§f These blocks §bcarry items§f along their path.§e When something drops nearby, the belt §agrabs it automatically§f. §bEjectors§e also feed items onto them.§f Items get pulled off by special tools called §binserters§f.

§e§bYellow§e ones move stuff §fone after another§f. §e§bRed§e versions carry §6two lines at once§f. §e§bPurple§e handles §6three lanes together§f. §e§bGreen§e runs §afaster than the rest§f. §eItems stay spaced out while riding them§f.

§6Mass Storage Units (MSUs)§f are storage blocks designed to hold a §6massive quantity§f of §ba single item§f. They are ideal for §aautocrafting setups§f and other systems that constantly consume common materials.

§eEach MSU can store only §cone item type§f, but offers §6far higher capacity§f than standard storage options, keeping automation lines §asteady and accessible§f.

§bWood MSU§f is crafted using §68 wooden planks§f of any type and an §6iron plate§f. Higher-tier MSUs are made from §brarer materials§f such as §6steel§f, §6desh§f, and §6cadmium/technetium steel§f, and often require §belectronic components§f to assemble.

§6Multi Fluid Identifier§f is an §6advanced fluid filter§f that allows you to §aselect the target fluid§f directly, without crafting multiple §bFluid Identifiers§f from the §bTemplate Folder§f. This greatly §areduces setup overhead§f in complex fluid systems and is especially useful in §elarge-scale processing lines§f.

§6Settings Tool§f is a utility item used to §6copy and apply machine configurations§f. It allows you to duplicate §einputs, outputs, and behavior settings§f between machines, making it §aindispensable§f when building §6large refineries§f or repeated machine arrays.
betterquesting:10.name:8: Multi Fluid Identifier and Settings Tool

betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Bobmazon§f is a §bshopping book§f that allow you to §6purchase materials§f, §bweapons§f, §bmachines§f, and more. It features §65 standard levels§f and §62 secret levels§f.

§eEach level is unlocked by completing a §6specific advancement§f. The §bicon shown under each item§f indicates which advancement is required. §aLevels can be unlocked in any order§f.

§6Unlock Requirements§f

§bLevel 1§f – §eCreate a §6Blast Furnace§f  
§bLevel 2§f – §eCreate an §6Assembly Machine§f  
§bLevel 3§f – §eCreate a §6Chemical Plant§f  
§bLevel 4§f – §eProduce §6Desh§f  
§bLevel 5§f – §eProduce a §6Technetium Steel Ingot§f

§6Rotary Furnace§f is a §6large, steam-powered furnace§f used for §6advanced metal processing§f. It unlocks §bexclusive alloy recipes§f and is the §conly method§f for producing §6Saturnite§f.

§eThe machine requires both §bSteam§f and §bsolid fuel§f to operate.§f While active, it converts §bsolid and fluid inputs§f into §6molten metal§f, which can then be §ecast using basins§f, similar to the §bCrucible§f and §bArc Furnace§f.

§eThe Rotary Furnace is the §6primary production method§f for special alloys such as §6Saturnite§f and §6Gunmetal§f. It also offers §aalternative recipes§f for §bSteel§f and §bDesh§f, providing more flexibility in mid and late-game metallurgy.

§6Boiler§f is a §6heat-driven processing machine§f used to §6heat fluids§f for §bsteam generation§f and §boil refining§f. It requires an §6external heat source§f (such as a §bFirebox§f) placed §eunderneath§f to operate.

§eThe Boiler is §6essential for progression§f, especially for §boil processing§f. It contains a §616,000 mB input tank§f and an output tank whose size depends on how much the fluid §eexpands when heated§f.

§cThe Boiler will explode§f if its §coutput tank becomes full§f.  
§a(The Industrial Boiler does not explode.)§f

§6Operation:§f

§ePlace a heat source below the Boiler§f, then §9right-click with a §bFluid Identifier§f to select which fluid to process (for example, §bCrude Oil§f).

§eThe Boiler has §63 fluid ports§f:
§b• One on top§f  
§b• Two on the sides§f  

§eHeat transfer rate§f: §6ΔT × 0.01 TU/t§f

§6Steam Handling:§f

§eWhen used by machines§f (such as §bReactors§f or §bChemical Factories§f), Steam exits as §bLow-Pressure Steam§f. This can be §arecycled at 100% efficiency§f back into water using a §bSteam Condenser§f.

§aClosed-loop water systems are strongly recommended§f, especially for reactors.

§6Applications:§f

§b• Heating Crude, Cracked, or Desulfurized Oil§f for use in an §bOil Refinery§f  
§b• Boiling Water§f to produce §6Steam§f  
§b• Converting Steam§f into §eDense Steam or other variants§f, when applicable  

§eAfter Steam completes power generation§f, remaining heat and pressure are §cinsufficient for reuse§f. §bSteam Condensers§f release this waste heat and condense Steam back into water.

§6Solar Tower Boiler§f uses §6direct sunlight§f to boil §bwater§f into §bsteam§f, functioning only during §edaytime§f. To operate, it requires §bHeliostat Mirrors§f to redirect sunlight into the tower.

§eHeliostat Mirrors must be aimed at the boiler using a §9Mirror Adjustment Tool§f.§f  
§9Right-click§f the boiler to store its position, then §9right-click§f mirrors to link and aim them automatically.

§eMirror behavior:
§b• Maximum range§f: §6100 blocks§f (no efficiency loss with distance)  
§b• Rotation limit§f: §645°§f  
§eHigher placement of the boiler allows §amore mirrors§f to be linked effectively.

§eAt full sunlight§f, the boiler consumes §61 mB of water§f per §612.5 mirrors§f each tick and produces §6100 mB of steam§f per mB of water used. §eWater consumption is rounded down to whole millibuckets§f.

§eTheoretical maximum steam output§f is §610,000 mB/t§f, though actual output is §climited by internal water and steam buffers§f.

§6Steam Engine:§f

§eSteam Engines convert §bsteam§f into §6electric power.§f

§eThey operate at §685% efficiency§f, outperforming §bStirling Engines§f. However, throughput is §climited§f by an internal capacity of §62000 mB of steam§f.

§eAt maximum rate§f, a Steam Engine processes §62000 mB/t§f, producing approximately §63400 HE/t§f (§668 kHE/s§f).

§eWith §6Casting§f unlocked, you can now produce §bkey firearm components§f such as §bbarrels§f and §bgrips§f. These parts form the §6foundation§f of most weapon assemblies.

§6Ammo Press§f is used to manufacture §bgun ammunition§f. Different ammo types require §especific materials and molds§f, making this machine §6essential§f for maintaining a reliable weapons supply.

§6Mining Charges§f are explosives designed specifically for §6ore extraction§f. Unlike regular blasts, they §ado not destroy drops§f, making them ideal for early deep mining before proper tools are available.

§eA standard mining charge explodes instantly when powered by §credstone§f. It affects an area of about §e3 blocks§f and §adoes not harm entities§f, focusing only on breaking terrain.

§eThere are §6three variants§f of mining charges:

§b• Mining Charge§f – §eInstant detonation, basic mining explosive§f.  
§b• Timed Mining Charge§f – §eDelayed explosion, easier and safer to place§f.  
§b• Semtex Mining Charge§f – §cStronger blast§f with §6Fortune III§f applied to drops.

§eThe §bTimed Mining Charge§e offers no extra mining bonuses beyond its delay, while the §bSemtex variant§e trades cost for §ahigher yield§f.

§eCrafting options vary by materials available:
§b• Mining Charges§f can be made using §6flint§f with §bmetal plates§f and §bdynamite§f, or upgraded recipes using §bsemtex§f or §bC-4§f, which are §amore efficient§f and produce §612 charges at once§f.
§b• Timed Mining Charges§f are crafted from a §6time bomb§f and §bflint§f.
§b• Semtex Mining Charges§f are assembled using §bsticks of semtex§f and §bduct tape§f.

The Comforts mod introduces §asleeping bags§f and §ahammocks§f to Minecraft.
These items §afunction similarly to beds§f, but do not change the player's spawn place, allowing them to §aremain at home base§f.
They are available in §b16 different colors§f, exactly like beds!
§aSleeping bags§f can be §alayered§f, making them ideal for adventures, whilst §ahammocks§f serve as a bed for the day, transitioning between day and night.§f

§eTo make a hammock§f, you'll need some §bhammock cloth§f. You'll also need §6two pairs of rope and nails§f.
You now need to §bfind some blocks that are exactly four spaces apart§f.
Once you've selected an appropriate position, §barrange the two pairs of rope and nails§f on the logs, facing each other.
Now, grab your §bhammock cloth§f and §9right-click§f one of the ropes or nails to take a quick nap!

Realistic Torches complicates early gameplay by §ccausing torches to burn out after 1 minecraft day (20 minutes), 
§f§b§aprompting players to switch to more permanent light sources early on.§f

To§b light a torch §fafter crafting, §buse a matchbox or flint and steel.§f

§cTorches are extinguished when rain occurs and water comes in contact with it.§f

§bGlowstone can be used to craft torches that are permanently lighted.§f

§aLight-emitting blocks and items will emit light when held.

Zombie Awareness makes zombies §6more intelligent§f overall. Here are some of the things you need to be aware of:

§c⁕ Blood Scents§f: §aTaking damage causes you to spill blood§f, and if your health is low, you'll §coccasionally bleed out§f.

§c⁕ Sound Sources attract zombies§f: §aMining blocks generates noise§f, alerting nearby zombies to your presence.

§c⁕ Light Sources also attract zombies§f: §aExposed light sources can be seen from a great distance§f, drawing zombies to investigate if they have a §eclear line of sight§f.

§c⁕ Group Spawning at night§f: §aExpect zombies to spawn in groups§f during the night, leading to §crandom invasions§f.

§c⁕ Wandering Hordes at night§f: §aLarge groups of mobs will roam the terrain§f unpredictably at night.

§c⁕ Faster Zombies§f: §aZombies may randomly receive a speed boost§f, making their movement §cunpredictable§f.

§6Gunpowder. Spice. Everything nice.§f  
The usual recipe for something harmless.

§4Then someone added §4Chemical X§f.§f  
§cBecause of course they did.§f

§eThe result wasn’t cute.§f  
§4Mutant Creatures§f now roam the world, and they are §cvery much your problem§f.

§eMutant versions§f of §bZombies§f, §bSkeletons§f, §bEndermen§f, §bCreepers§f, and §bSnow Golems§f spawn naturally, each with §cunpleasant surprises§f and §6enhanced abilities§f.

§cIf that wasn’t enough§f, regular mobs can also spawn wearing §bNTM: CE armor§f and carrying §bNTM: CE weapons§f. §eThey look normal§f. §cThey are not.§f Some are equipped well enough to §4end bad decisions instantly§f.

§aSmile.§f  
§eThe world is alive.§f  
§cAnd it wants you dead.§f

Pam's HarvestCraft significantly expands Minecraft's §6food system§f, adding:

§6Ingredients§f: §280 new crops§f (like cotton), §226 garden bushes§f, and §250 fruit/item trees§f.
§6Resources§f: §219 new fish§f, plus a §2beekeeping system§f for items like §2candles§f, §2wax§f, and §2honey§f.
§6Variety§f: Over §21400 new foods and items§f, along with §are-balanced vanilla recipes§f.
§6Utility Blocks§f: Includes §2placeable cakes§f, a §2Presser§f for resource production (§2honey§f, §2paper§f), a §2Market§f for §2buying seeds/spawners§f, §2Shipping§f for selling goods, and §2Ground/Water Traps§f for catching resources with bait.
§6Streamlined Gameplay§f: §2Plant crops directly using items§f, convert water/milk to fresh versions easily, use tofu for vegetarian recipes, and §9right-click§f to harvest and replant crops.

§6Cooking for Blockheads§f complements this with a §2cooking book§f and a §2functional kitchen§f.
Use the book or §2Cooking Table§f to quickly find recipes based on your ingredients and cook with §aone click§f.
§bThe modular kitchen§f can be upgraded for extra storage or an oven.
You can even capture a §bcow in a jar§f (using an anvil, cow, and jar) for §6infinite milk§f.

You can use the §7§8§9/rtp §fcommand to randomly teleport around the world. This will §a§e§areally come in handy when finding HBM structures to loot§f§a.§f
All you need to do before this is §cset your home using §9/home§f (not required if you dont want your home near to spawn and are finding for one using rtp)
and you're set to go!

§aYou can also use the §9/back §acommand to teleport to your previous location in case you want to use it.

§6The Chisel mod significantly enhances the decorative options in Minecraft:§f

§aBlocks meant for looks show up in big numbers here.§f A bunch of fresh options appear, changing what walls or floors might become. For those focused on design, choices multiply fast.
§6Chisel Tool§f: §eMost new blocks are accessed through a single tool, the §bChisel§f.
§aFans inside factory zones stretch their look across several blocks.§f These special surfaces §acover more than one block at a time§f.
§eShifting textures just got easier§f — the §bChisel tool§f moves them in any direction with accuracy. Position tweaks happen smoothly, whether vertical or horizontal adjustments are needed. Direction changes stay exact, thanks to its responsive design.
§eA step up from the basics§f — §6iChisel§f brings §aadditional functions§f that expand beyond standard use.

§eThanks to §6ConnectedTexturesMod§f, textures can flow naturally between blocks. Surfaces that once looked disconnected now §ablend together§f as the mod quietly adjusts visuals behind the scenes.
§eFine ridges and details§f stand out more clearly due to §6enhanced texture designs§f that add depth to surfaces.

In this world of Minecraft, you find that it has become §cvery hard§f to break trees by punching with your hand!

Instead, §bgo and dig some §9Gravel§b until you get some §9flint§b. §9Right-click§f with the flint in your hand onto any kind of §9Stone block§b, and you get some §9Flint shards§b!
§bUse these Flint shards§f with some sticks to make a §9flint knife§f.

Using the knife, you can get some §9Plant Fibres§f which will be useful to make all sorts of §9flint tools§f such as Pickaxes, Hatchets, etc., that also use sticks and strings that can be obtained easily.

So to get §9planks§f from §9wood§f, you need to grab a §9flint hatchet§f and put the logs on the ground.
Once done, §9right-click§f the top of the wood with the hatchet to get §9planks§f.

§a§fThis mod adds graves that spawn with your stuff upon your death. Here are a few features:

§aItems return to the exact same slot they were in when you died.

Graves adapt to their surroundings!

Grief-resistant! §fOnly the person who died is able to retrieve their items §a(unless they grant access through the built-in friend system).§f 

§aBlocks are also virtually indestructible§f and§a can be locked §fso that you can't accidentally retrieve items before you're ready! 

A list of items that were on your person upon death will be in your inventory after you die and respawn.

One thing that you can do before engaging in the progression, as well as something you should always do if you have the time, is§6 looting structures.§f

§cYou shouldn't solely rely on structures for your resources, §f§abut they give you a head-start when it comes to progressing through the mod, as well as the modpack.
§f
§a§a§2§4§5§6§7§8§9§2§a§bHere's a list of structures which are great for raiding in early-game:

§9Antennas: §fA common structure with a few resources, mainly §9steel ingots, wires and analog circuits.§f

§9Radio stations:§f A great source of §9concrete §fand §9steel,§f the small side building also has §9tanks filled with diesel.§f
The chests often also contain §9revolvers §fand sometimes §9weaker shotguns §fwhich are fantastic alternatives to your stinky old vanilla bow.

§9Relay stations:§f Huge antenna-like structures with§9 loads and loads of steel.§f
§bSteel sets are only marginally better than iron§f - but with steel so easily accessible and in such quantities,§a you'll be able to make steel tools quickly.

§9Crashed space ships: §fSomewhat§6 rare§f and §bfilled with items that aren't very useful in early game,§f they still contain a §9considerable amount of steel §fand a §6chest full of rare items,§f more often than§9 not consumables§f and§9 rocket launchers.

Crashed vertibirds: §fHelicopters that are buried in the desert, they are a §9good source of steel §fand §9often contain parts for power armor.

Factories: §fLarge structures made of brick which have §9many machines §fand§f§9 chests full of raw materials.
betterquesting:10.name:8: Things to do before you start

betterquesting:10.desc:8:
Iron Backpacks revolutionizes portable storage with these features:

§2Tiered backpacks come in four levels §f- each unlocks extra perks like §6expanded space §for §6added upgrade options §fas you climb up. Higher tiers trade basic function for smarter features through gradual progression. One step up brings roomier builds; another favors customization depth. Benefits shift subtly, matching how you use them rather than pushing one-size-fits-all promises.
§2Visual Equipping§f:§f Backpacks can be equipped and will appear on your character model.
§2Swap in fresh features anytime§f - build your pack with more than twenty one-of-a-kind tweaks.
§aThese upgrades bring smarter ways to collect items, refill stock levels, then drop things into storage fast - just a few of countless traits.

§6Crucible Smelting Setup:

§eThe §6Crucible§f is a §6core early-game machine§f in NTM. Many materials §ccannot be processed any other way§f, making it essential to learn early. This guide walks you through building a §6basic foundry setup§f and casting §bCast Copper Plates§f—your first unavoidable crucible product.

§6Prerequisites§f

§eBefore smelting, you must craft a §6Crucible§f using a §bSteel Anvil§f. While the anvil itself is simple, all foundry machines require §6Firebricks§f.

§eFirebricks are smelted from §bFireclay§f, which can be crafted in two ways:
§b• 3 Clay§f + §b1 Aluminum Powder / Ore§f  
§b• 2 Clay§f + §b1 Sand§f + §b1 Limestone§f (§asaves aluminum§f)

§eTo build the §6simplest foundry setup§f, you will need:
§b• 1× Crucible§f  
§b• 1× Heating Oven or Firebox§f  
§b• 1× Shallow Foundry Basin§f  
§e  (Use a normal basin for blocks, steel pipes, or 9× molds)  
§b• 1× Blank Foundry Mold§f  
§b• Raw material to cast§f (Copper)  
§b• Any furnace fuel§f (Coal, wood, etc.)

§6Step 1 – Creating the Foundry Mold§f

§eA §bBlank Foundry Mold§f must be shaped before use. To do this, place:
§b• The desired output item§f in slot one of §eany anvil§f  
§b• A Blank Foundry Mold§f in slot two

§eFor this example, a §bCast Iron Plate§f is used to create a §6Cast Plate Mold§f.

§eYou may notice that this requires an existing cast plate.§f This is intentional—§bCast Iron Plates§f have a §esimple but inefficient§f crafting recipe specifically to bootstrap crucible usage.

§6Step 2 – Building the Foundry§f

§eClear a §64×3 block area§f for the setup. Leaving §a1 block of space§f around the crucible is strongly recommended for access.

§eAssembly order:
§b• Place the Heating Oven or Firebox§f on the ground  
§b• Place the Crucible§f directly §eon top§f of it  
§b• Move to the back (green output side)§f  
§b• Place the Shallow Foundry Basin§f on the ground below the green channel  
§b• Right-click the basin with the mold§f to insert it

§eTo change molds later§f, either §ebreak the basin§f or use a §bScrewdriver§f to eject it.

§aThis completes the basic foundry setup.§f

§6Step 3 – Operating the Foundry§f

§eInsert §bany furnace fuel§f into the Heating Oven or Firebox.§f Do not worry about wasted fuel—§athe system does not lose TU passively§f.

§eNext, place your §braw material§f (Copper) into the §69 internal slots§f of the Crucible. After a short delay, it will begin §6melting into molten metal§f.

§eOnce enough metal accumulates§f (§63 ingots for cast plates§f), the Crucible will §aautomatically pour§f into the mold.

§eAfter a brief cooldown§f, the metal solidifies. §9Right-click the basin§f to retrieve the finished item.

§aThe process then repeats automatically§f as long as material and heat are available.

§6Steel Anvil (Tier 2)§f is an §6upgraded iron anvil§f that expands on the basic iron anvil by §aunlocking additional recipes§f.

§eThis tier marks a step into §6stronger materials§f and §emore complex components§f, enabling the creation of machines and parts that are §cnot possible§f with lower-tier anvils.

§eWhile it functions the same as previous anvils§f, its value comes from §6broader recipe access§f, making it a §akey progression checkpoint§f as you move deeper into NTM:CE.

§6Pollution§f is divided into §63 subtypes§f, all of which can be §etracked using a pollution monitor§f.

§bSoot§f is the §emost common§f form, produced by §6fossil fuel combustion§f and related processes. It §cincreases mob health and strength§f, and at high levels becomes §evisible as smog§f.

§bPoison§f builds up in certain environments and §cdamages unprotected entities§f once concentrations rise too high.

§bHeavy Metal§f pollution comes from burning §6leaded fuels§f. It §cincreases mob strength§f but will also §charm mobs§f at extreme levels. §cPlayers and entities without gas protection are affected§f.

§eMany machines can be connected to §bspecial exhaust pipes§f, which act as §6dedicated pollution ducts§f. These can route output into a §bchimney (§bsmokestack)§f to §areduce environmental impact§f.

§eDead dirt can be restored§f by spraying §bseeding slurry§f using a §bchemthrower§f, allowing polluted land to §arecover into grass§f.

§6Automatic Buzz Saw§f is an automation machine designed to §6harvest trees.§. It operates on §bburnable oils§f instead of electricity, making it suitable for early-to-mid game setups.

§eWhen active§f, the Buzz Saw will §6chop down trees§f within a §610-block radius§f and §areplant saplings automatically§f. It also supports §bfarming operations§f, harvesting crops and §areplanting seeds§f without player input.

§eThis machine is commonly used to automate renewable resources§f such as §bwood§f, §blatex§f, §bhemp§f, and §bmustard willows§f, providing a §asteady supply§f of basic materials for processing chains.

§6Industrial Mixer§f is a processing machine used to §6combine fluids§f with §bitems§f or other §bfluids§f. It overlaps with some §bChemical Plant§f recipes and can act as a §apractical alternative§f where applicable.

§eThe mixer’s interface contains §63 input slots§f:
§b• Two fluid inputs§f  
§b• One item input§f  

§eThe output side accepts a §bFluid Identifier§f, which §6locks the active recipe§f. Without a valid identifier, the mixer §cwill not process§f.

§eCertain fluids support §6alternate recipes§f. These can be selected using the §9recipe toggle button§f on the input side. Notable multi-recipe fluids include:

§b• Fracking Solution§f  
§e  – Water + Petroleum Gas + Sulfur  
§e  – Sulfuric Acid + Petroleum Gas  

§b• Biofuel§f  
§e  – Wood Oil + Fish Oil  
§e  – Wood Oil + Sunflower Seed Oil  

§b• Nitroglycerin§f  
§e  – Nitric Acid + Petroleum Gas  
§e  – Nitric Acid + Fish Oil  

§b• Oxyhydrogen§f  
§e  – Liquid Hydrogen + Liquid Oxygen  
§e  – Liquid Hydrogen only  

§b• Engine Lubricant§f  
§e  – Ethanol + Sunflower Seed Oil  
§e  – Ethanol + Fish Oil  
§e  – Heating Oil + Unsaturated Hydrocarbons  

§b• Solvent§f  
§e  – Aromatic Hydrocarbons + any §64 types of Naphtha§f  

§eThe Industrial Mixer supports §emany additional recipes§f beyond those listed here. For a complete reference, consult §9JEI§f.

§6Player Decontaminator§f is a utility block used to §6remove radiation exposure§f while a player is standing on it. It offers a §acost-efficient alternative§f to consumables like §bRadAway§f, which require constant replacement.

§eWhile standing on a Decontaminator§f, the player loses §610 RAD/s§f. If present, the §c“Contaminated”§f status effect is also §aremoved§f over time.

§eEfficiency can be increased§f by standing at the §6center of four Decontaminators§f, which §6quadruples the radiation removal rate§f, making it ideal for §aQuick radiation Decontamination.

§6Energy Storage Block§f is the §6most basic power buffer§f available. The standard variant can store up to §61 MHE§f and is used to §astore and distribute electricity§f.

§eEnergy Storage Blocks can power§f §bmachines§f, §bcharge batteries§f, and supply §btools§f such as the §bImpact Drill§f, making them a §6core part§f of any electrical setup.

§eCharging methods§f:
§b• Insert a charged battery§f  
§b• Connect via §bRed Copper Cable§f to a §bpower source§f (such as a Wood-Burning Generator)

§6Power Control§f

§eEach storage block has a §6transfer mode§f that controls how energy flows:
§b• Input§f – only receives power  
§b• Output§f – only supplies power  
§b• Buffer§f – both receives and outputs power  
§b• None§f – disables all power transfer  

§eTransfer modes can be §6toggled via redstone§f, allowing automated control.

§6Energy Priority§f

§eStorage blocks also support §6priority settings§f, which determine how power is distributed:
§b• Low Priority§f (§edefault§f) – §eMachines fill first§f, storage charges last  
§b• Medium Priority§f – §eStorage and machines charge equally§f  
§b• High Priority§f – §cStorage fills first§f, machines receive power only afterward  

§aProper use of priorities§f helps stabilize power networks and §aprevent brownouts§f in larger systems.

§6Machine Upgrade Template§f is the §6foundation component§f required to create all §bmachine upgrades§f. No upgrade can be produced without it.

§eMachine upgrades serve more than just efficiency gains§f. While some improve §6speed, power usage, or output§f, others §aunlock entirely new machine functions§f, changing how a machine operates or what it can do.

§eAs progression advances§f, upgrades become §6mandatory§f for automation, optimization, and accessing §chigher-tier mechanics§f.

§6Drainage Pipe§f is a utility block used to §6vent fluids and gases§f directly into the §cenvironment§f. Any material dumped this way is §cpermanently lost§f and converted into §6pollution§f.

§eThis block is typically used§f when storage or processing options are unavailable. While effective at clearing excess buildup, §cfrequent use will rapidly increase local pollution levels§f.

§eDrainage Pipes should be used §ccarefully§f and §esparingly§f, as improper disposal can §ccontaminate areas with pollution§f over time.

§6Electric Furnace§f is a §6basic RF-powered smelting machine§f that §eoperates similarly to a vanilla furnace§f, while allowing §afull automation§f within §bRF-based ore processing chains§f. This makes it well-suited for §6continuous, hands-off smelting§f.

§eAlthough its base smelting speed§f is comparable to standard furnaces, the Electric Furnace can be §aenhanced§f using §bSpeed§f and §bEnergy Upgrades§f to improve both §6processing time§f and §6power efficiency§f.

§6Ingot Former§f is a §6processing machine§f used to §6form ingots or gems§f from §bmolten materials§f produced by other §bNuclearCraft machines.§f

§eThe Ingot Former§f is typically placed at the §eend of molten alloy production chains§f, that make use of §bMelters§f and §bFluid Mixers§f to §6cool and solidify liquids§f into §busable ingots§f for crafting and machine construction, apart from its other uses.

§eBy transitioning away from crucible-based processing§f and toward §bmelter-driven automation§f, the Ingot Former helps §astreamline metallurgy§f in later stages, making it an §aimportant utility§f for fully automated NuclearCraft setups.

§6Redstone-Controlled Lights§f provide configurable lighting options, with optional §bredstone§f control for automation setups.

§eThe basic light§f requires §ano RF power§f and emits light equivalent to §bGlowstone§f. In addition, there are §6two advanced tiers§f that illuminate a §alarger area§f than Glowstone but §crequire RF§f to operate.

§eLights are available in §6two form factors§f:
§b• Flat (panel) variant§f  
§b• Full block variant§f  

§eEach variant supports §65 different colors§f, allowing flexible integration into both §abuilds§f and §etechnical layouts§f.

§6Fluid Infuser§f is a processing machine used to §6infuse items with fluids§f, altering their properties or converting them into §bnew components§f.

§eThe machine consumes a specified §bfluid§f and applies it to an §bitem input§f based on the selected recipe. This process is commonly used in §6advanced material processing§f and §bnuclear-related crafting chains§f.

§eFluid Infusion§f is required for certain §cprogression-critical recipes§f that cannot be completed using standard crafting or alloying methods.

§6Machine Interface§f is an auxiliary block used to §6extend the input and output sides§f of an adjacent §bNuclearCraft machine§f.

§eWhen placed next to a machine§f, the interface acts as an §aadditional access face§f, allowing §bitems, fluids, or power§f to be inserted or extracted as if interacting with the machine itself.

§eThis is especially useful§f for §6compact builds§f, complex automation layouts, or machines with §climited exposed sides§f, as it provides greater flexibility without altering machine behavior.

§6Voltaic Pile§f is a §6low-cost RF energy storage block§f intended for §eearly-game power setups§f. It provides an §ainternal buffer of 160 kRF§f, making it a solid option before §bLithium-based energy storage§f becomes available.

§eAlthough its capacity is limited§f, the Voltaic Pile is §aeasy to craft§f and works well with §6basic machines§f that rely on §ebasic and advanced plating§f.

§eUsing a §bMultitool§f, the Voltaic Pile can be §aconfigured§f to control its input and output sides. §cNote§f: the tool configures the §eopposite face§f of the side you are interacting with.

§eWhen paired with §bLeadstone Fluxducts§f, the Voltaic Pile can be integrated into §blarge RF power networks.§f

§6Crystallizer§f is a §6processing machine§f used to §6extract solid items from fluids§f through controlled precipitation.

§eThe Crystallizer§f consumes §bfluid inputs§f and converts them into §bsolid products§f such as §bgems, salts, or crystals§f, depending on the recipe. It is commonly used in §bchemical§f and §bRadAway processing§f chains where materials must be recovered from liquid form.

§eThis machine§f is typically used alongside §bChemical Reactors§f, usually at the end of processing chains, completing fluid-based processing loops by turning §bprocessed liquids§f back into §ausable solids§f.

The Alloy Furnace is used to make alloys, as well as being a crafting component in the recipe for a bunch of other machines.§6Alloy Furnace§f is a §6basic processing machine§f used to §6combine multiple materials into alloys§f. It serves as the §eentry point§f for alloy production in NuclearCraft.

§eThe Alloy Furnace processes§f §bitems only§f (no fluids) and is primarily used to create §6foundational alloys§f required for early and mid-game progression. Many of these alloys are §cmandatory§f for unlocking more advanced machines and systems.

§eIn addition to its processing role§f, the Alloy Furnace is also a §6key component§f in unlocking §bmultiple higher-tier machines§f, making it an §aessential early investment§f even after its direct alloy recipes are no longer in heavy use.

§6Decay Hastener§f uses §bRF power§f to §6accelerate radioactive decay§f processes. It is primarily used to §eprocess decay-based materials faster§f, reducing wait times in isotope handling chains.

§eBy supplying RF§f, the Decay Hastener speeds up reactions that would otherwise occur §cnaturally and slowly§f, making it a §6time-saving utility§f for nuclear progression rather than a direct resource generator.

§6Welcome to NuclearCraft!§f  
This mod focuses on §6large-scale power generation§f through §bNuclear Fission§f and §bNuclear Fusion§f.

§eThe most challenging aspect§f is producing the required §6nuclear fuels and isotopes§f. While §bJEI§f documents the full process, many materials involve §emulti-step production chains§f that must be followed carefully.

§8Some quest descriptions are adapted from the Multiblock Madness modpack.§f

§6Nuclear Furnace§f uses §bUranium§f or §bThorium Ingots§f as fuel to §6rapidly smelt items§f. These fuels burn §asignificantly faster§f than conventional sources, making the furnace ideal for §ehigh-throughput smelting§f at the cost of §cradioactive materials§f.

§6Speed Upgrade§f reduces the §6processing time§f of machines by making them run faster, at the cost of §cincreased power consumption§f.

§4Note§f: §eSome machines will reset their progress§f if they §crun out of power§f mid-operation, making stable energy supply important when using Speed Upgrades.

§6Energy Upgrade§f lowers the §6energy cost§f required per operation, helping machines §arun more efficiently§f. However, the number of Energy Upgrades §ccannot exceed§f the number of §bSpeed Upgrades§f installed.

§eUsing Energy Upgrades§f makes it §aeasier to keep machines running continuously§f, especially in high-throughput setups.

§eBoth upgrades§f are crafted using §6Research Points§f, which are obtained from the §bResearcher multiblock§f.

§6Li-Ion Cell§f is a §6portable energy storage item§f capable of storing up to §6800 kRF§f.

§eThe cell can be §acharged directly§f by placing it inside an §bHBM Energy Storage Block§f, allowing seamless integration between §bRF-based systems§f and HBM power infrastructure.

§eOnce charged§f, the Li-Ion Cell can be used to §asupply RF§f to compatible machines or systems, making it a §6convenient buffer§f for mobile or intermediate power needs.

§6Melter§f is a §6processing machine§f used to §6convert solid items§f such as §bingots, dusts, or components§f into their §bmolten liquid forms§f.

§eThe Melter§f serves as the §eentry point§f for molten processing chains, supplying §bmolten materials§f to machines like the §bFluid Mixer§f for alloying or directly to the §bIngot Former§f for solidification.

§eThis machine§f replaces traditionalcrucible-based melting in §6advanced metallurgy§f, enabling §aprecise, fully automated§f liquid-based production systems essential for late-game NuclearCraft setups.

§6Fluid Mixer§f is a §6fluid-processing machine§f used to §6combine molten liquids§f into §bnew molten alloys§f or §bmolten compounds§f.

§eThe Fluid Mixer§f works by taking §btwo fluid inputs§f and producing a §6single output fluid§f. It is commonly used in §bmolten alloy production chains§f, as well as in §bMolten Salt Reactor (MSR)§f workflows for §bFLiBe-based nuclear salts§f and §bNaK-based molten coolants§f, among other applications.

§eThis machine§f is a §6core component§f of molten liquid processing setups, typically operating alongside the §bMelter§f and §bIngot Former§f to enable §afully automated alloy and compound production§f.

§6RF to HE conversion§f is now §aseamless§f. You can directly connect §bThermal Dynamics power cables§f to an §bHBM Energy Storage Block§f to convert and distribute power.

§eOnce connected§f, RF is automatically converted and supplied as §6HE§f to any compatible machine on the network.

§eCurrent conversion rate§f: §61 RF = 1 HE§f.

§6Pressurizer§f is a §6processing machine§f that uses §6high pressure§f to transform items into §bcompressed or altered forms§f, including §bgems§f and certain plate variants.

§eIn practice§f, the Pressurizer has §climited applications§f outside of §bgem production§f from powdered materials, as plates production is covered by HBM §banvils.§f These gems are §6important components§f in a variety of §bNuclearCraft-related recipes§f, making the machine §asituational but necessary§f for specific progression paths.

§6RF to HE Conversion§f is now fully §aseamless§f. By connecting §bThermal Dynamics power cables§f directly to an §bHBM Energy Storage Block§f, §bRF§f is automatically converted into §bHE§f at a §61:1 ratio§f, with no energy loss.

§eFor reliable operation§f, it is recommended to use a §bVoltaic Pile§f and a §bMultitool§f to configure the interfaces correctly. Set the §bHE side§f to §6INPUT§f and the §bRF side§f to §6OUTPUT§f.

§cImportant§f: The §bHBM Energy Storage Block§f must be set to §6OUTPUT ONLY§f using the §eleft-side mode button§f (next to the blue information icon, not the redstone control). If this setting is incorrect, §ctransfer will not occur§f.

§eOnce converted§f, RF-derived energy can be §adistributed normally§f to §bany compatible machine§f.

§eIn addition§f, §bHBM Energy Storage Blocks§f can §acharge RF-based items and blocks§f when placed inside them, allowing smooth integration between §bRF power systems§f and §bHBM energy networks§f without the need for extra converters. You can use this feature to charge your machines using a Lithium Ion Cell for now, until you get a voltaic pile.

§6Steam Transformer§f is a §6steam-era processing machine§f added by §bNC Steam Additions§f. It consumes §bsteam§f to §6process up to four item inputs§f into new outputs defined by its recipes.

§eUnlike standard NuclearCraft machines§f, the Steam Transformer does not use RF or HE. Instead, it relies entirely on §bsteam§f to produce §brefined components§f that are part of the steam progression tier.

§cWarning§f: There is a known issue where the Steam Transformer §ceats Speed Upgrades§f during operation. Until this bug is fixed, it is §cstrongly recommended§f to §eavoid installing upgrades§f in this machine.

§6Steam Compactor§f is a §6steam-powered processing machine§f used to §6compress items§f into denser or more refined forms.

§eThe machine consumes §bsteam§f, using pressure to §bcompress materials§f to make §bstuff like plates amongst other materials.§f This makes it a §6core utility§f in the steam age for producing components that cannot be crafted by hand or with standard machines.

§6Electrolyzer§f is a §6fluid-processing machine§f used to §6separate fluids§f into their §bconstituent components§f using electrical power.

§eThe Electrolyzer§f takes a §bfluid input§f and splits it into §bmultiple output fluids§f based on its stoichiometric formula. It is commonly used in §bchemical processing§f chains where reactive fluids are required.

§eThis machine§f is a §6key component§f of advanced NuclearCraft setups, often working alongside the §bChemical Reactor§f to support §bcomplex reaction workflows§f and §bChemical production setups.

§6Rock Crusher§f is a §6processing machine§f used to §6crush solid blocks and items§f into §bdusts.§f

§eThe Rock Crusher§f is commonly used for §bore processing§f and §bmaterial preparation§f, breaking down raw materials into forms that can be §afurther used§f by machines such as the §bChemical Reactors.§f

§6Steam Boiler§f is a §6steam-generation machine§f used to §6convert water into steam§f using a §blow-tier/high-tier fuel§f. It serves as the §efoundation§f of all §bsteam-based machinery§f in the steam technology tier.

§eThe Steam Boiler requires§f a constant supply of §bwater§f and §bfuel§f to operate. Once active, it produces §bdifferent types of steam§f that can be routed into §bsteam-powered machines§f such as the §6Steam Transformer, Steam Compactor§f and other steam-based machines.

§eThis machine is §crequired§f to generate Nuclearcraft-steam to achieve §6steam-based progression.

§6Advanced Plating§f is a §6high-tier construction component§f used in the crafting of §badvanced machines§f such as §bCrystallizers, Melters, .

§eIt represents a step up§f from basic structural materials and is §6commonly required§f for mid-game NuclearCraft progression.

§eAdvanced Plating§f is primarily used as a §bcrafting material§f rather than a processing component, acting as a §6gatekeeper item§f for higher-complexity machinery and infrastructure.

§6Chemical Reactor§f is a §6fluid-based processing machine§f used to §6carry out chemical reactions§f between §bfluids.§f

§eThe Chemical Reactor§f is primarily used to produce §bchemical compounds§f that cannot be obtained through basic mixing or smelting.

§eThis machine§f is a §6core component§f of §badvanced NuclearCraft processing chains§f, especially in §bMSR fuel preparation, Variety of chemicals production chains,§f and §bRadAway production setups.
The Dawn of Civilization
Refine oil to create plastics using byproducts, advanced circuitry and so much more.
Nuclear age lets you advance into Realistic Nuclear reactors, as well as more advanced machinery.
Delve into Hypothetical Physics, Quantum Computing, Magic and maybe even destroy the world, who knows?
Steam is the base source of literally any power production method, while of course HX adds an extra step using a coolant....
